sections.paragraph:
Φιλομήλιον, ου, τό (Strabo 12, 8, 14; Ptolem. 5, 2, 25 al.) Philomelium, a city in Phrygia (central Asia Minor), not far fr. Antioch in Pisidia: MPol ins.—Pauly-W. XIX 2520–23; Kl. Pauly IV 769.

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ptolem Ptolem , math., II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. fr. = from

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Pauly-W Pauly-W = AP./GW., Real-Encyclopädie der klassischen Altertumswissenschaft—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1058
